query: "gas prices" OR "gasoline prices" OR "oil prices" OR petrol

articles.title: Asian stocks extend gains as US rebounds, chipmakers lead
articles.description: Stocks jumped at ​the start of trading in Asia on Wednesday as investors took cues from a rebound in U.S. ‌markets, shrugging off climbing oil prices as Houthi rebels threatened to open a front in the widening Middle East conflict.

articles.title: Middle East Tensions Roil Financial Markets Amid Renewed Conflict
articles.description: Tensions in the Middle East, particularly due to renewed U.S-Iran conflict, have once again rattled financial markets. As both Washington and Tehran refuse to back down, investors are cautious, and economic implications are severe. Rising oil prices and stagflation risks pose major challenges for policymakers across the globe.

articles.title: Global Market Today: Asian stocks advance on tech rally, yen weakens
articles.description: Asian stocks climbed for a second day, led by chipmakers. The Kospi Index saw a significant jump, reflecting AI investment optimism. Crude oil prices advanced, reigniting inflation concerns and pushing Treasury yields higher. Gold and silver also advanced on dip-buying, while the yen slid past 163 per dollar. Investors now await earnings from Alphabet and Tesla for further market direction.

articles.title: Currency Crossroads: Yen Hits Four-Decade Low Amid Global Market Shifts
articles.description: The yen has reached an almost four-decade low against the dollar as rising oil prices and U.S. Treasury yields bolster the greenback. Speculation surrounding possible Japanese intervention grows as the yen remains weak. The Middle East conflict continues to influence global markets, adding to the currency's volatility.

articles.title: Rupee rallies 21 paise to 96.24 against US dollar on lower oil prices and forex inflows
articles.description: The rupee strengthened by twenty-one paise, closing at ninety-six point twenty-four on Tuesday. Lower oil prices reduced demand for the US dollar, supporting the local currency. Dedicated programs aimed at drawing forex inflows have garnered nearly twenty-one billion dollars. State-run banks sold dollars, providing support around ninety-six point forty-two levels. The currency traded within a narrow range throughout the trading session.

articles.title: Inflation set to have cooled in June ahead of household energy squeeze
articles.description: Most economists think a sharp drop in petrol and diesel prices will have helped pull the overall inflation rate down.

articles.title: Pre-market action: Here's the trade setup for today's session
articles.description: Domestic equities extended losses for a second consecutive session on Tuesday. Higher crude oil prices and geopolitical tensions pressured the market significantly. Analysts expect equities to remain range-bound amid ongoing global uncertainties. The ongoing Q1 earnings season will drive stock-specific actions and investor sentiment. The Indian rupee firmed slightly as diplomacy hopes eased oil price concerns.

articles.title: Tensions in the Middle East: Oil Tankers Divert as Conflict Escalates
articles.description: Two Saudi oil tankers reversed course amidst threats from Yemen's Houthis as the Middle East conflict disrupts critical energy chokepoints. The U.S. continues bombing targets in Iran, with attacks spreading to Bahrain, Kuwait, and Jordan. Oil prices rise as shipping routes face potential closure.

articles.title: Oil prices rise slightly after US announces new round of strikes on Iran
articles.description: Oil prices inched higher in early trading on Wednesday as fears of further supply disruptions intensified after U.S. forces said it began striking ​Iranian military targets for the 11th straight night and Kuwait reported ‌attacks by Iranian drones.

articles.title: US strikes Iran for 11th night as Hormuz conflict derails ceasefire talks
articles.description: The US struck Iran for an 11th straight night as Pakistan tried to revive collapsed ceasefire talks. The fighting around the Strait of Hormuz is lifting oil prices and widening risks across the region.
